Abstract:
We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. Experiments on two machine translation tasks show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable.

Results:
Our model achieves 28.4 BLEU on English-to-German translation, improving by over 2.0 BLEU. The training cost was estimated to be 1e20 FLOPs.
As shown in Table 1, the training time is significantly reduced. Figure 1 shows the attention visualizations.
See Figure 2 for the model architecture. Table 2 outlines the BLEU scores.

Once upon a time, in a small cave high up in the Misty Mountains, there lived a little red
 dragon named Ignis.
 Ignis was not like the other dragons. While they loved hoarding gold and breathing fire at
 passing knights, Ignis preferred reading books and eating fresh red apples.
 He spent his days searching the valleys for wild apple trees.

One day, he met a friendly squirrel who showed him a secret orchard full of the sweetest,
 juiciest apples he had ever tasted.
 From that day on, Ignis was the happiest dragon in the mountains.
